entry.title: Re: Interested for SIT Kolkata 2024 ?
entry.content: Please use ID starting with 'P'

entry.title: Learn about Localization with SAP’s Experts at the DSAG-SAP Globalization Symposium 2024
entry.content:
Are you part of a global company confronted by mounting challenges on country/region-specific regulatory requirements? Are the obligations for electronic invoicing in various countries giving you sleepless nights? SAP provides peace of mind with a comprehensive portfolio for localization. And you will have a great opportunity to learn more about SAP’s latest updates for localization at the SAP German-Speaking User Group Symposium on Localization, to happen on April 22 and 23 in Sankt Leon-Rot, Germany.

Navigating through global regulations like VAT in the Digital Age (ViDA) and obligatory digital invoicing in countries like Poland, India, and China can be overwhelming. The DSAG-SAP-Globalization Symposium 2024 will provide you with more information about these and other current topics and enable insightful exchanges with the SAP community.

At the event, SAP experts will showcase the regulatory change manager for customers to stay up to date on regulatory changes and to plan actionable information on their SAP landscape and solutions. You will learn how localization as a self-service for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition helps you to expand SAP local versions as well as to create your own custom local versions. Moreover, you will learn about the extensibility of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for your company-specific requirements to optimize automation and minimize noncompliance risks while fulfilling real-time, periodic, and ad hoc mandates worldwide. Join the roundtables for each topic and learn from the experiences of SAP customers and partners.

Meet me on Monday, April 22, for a keynote talking about using SAP solutions for global business processes, addressing new regulatory trends and the central role of cloud-based solutions and new technologies such as SAP Business AI and Joule.

Besides the keynote and various lectures, you can attend the exhibition section of the event. Try out SAP’s new Official Document Numbering framework, meet SAP’s User Assistance experts onsite and join a usability testing for the regulatory change manager to support us in turning a good solution into a great one!

Learn more about the event and register now on the DSAG-SAP-Globalization-Symposium 2024 webpage.

The DSAG-SAP-Globalization Symposium 2024 is a great opportunity for SAP users to learn, network and exchange experiences. Irrespective of your membership with DSAG, use this chance to enhance your knowledge about localization. An immersive learning experience awaits you!

I am looking forward to meeting you there!

Note: All sessions will be conducted in German.

entry.title: Re: Interested for SIT Kolkata 2024 ?
entry.content: Yes it is team related only

entry.title: Re: create dynamically a type containing an include type
entry.content:
You do not need RTTI. You can do a

data: ldref type ref to data.

create data ldref type table of TY_ZTEST_123.

assign ldref->*to field-symbol(<myTable>).

in <my_table> you have a structured table. But the cl_gui_alv_grid accepts the ldref (set_table_for_first_display).

Hint: Do not use reuse alv. Better use cl_gui_alv_grid in your own container. This gives much more possibilities.

entry.title: Re: Credit Control area become blank if Credit limit is s...
entry.content: You can actually just set the currency and it will then allow you to enter 0.00, tried and tested.

entry.title: Re: Currency Translation during ML startup (CKMSTART)
entry.content: you can use the exchange rate type on the CKMSTART input screen. It can be the same exchange rate type which you maintain and use in OB08

entry.title: Re: Testing SAP Build Process Automation task UI in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
@MioYasutake 

 

Have you had a chance to try and call an Odata service? Can you try and see if you can get the Northwind service working in your example? I'm curious if you run into CORS errors.

entry.title: Re: create dynamically a type containing an include type
entry.content: The fact is the type will always have the same structure: include type( a table) and include type if_chdo_object_tools_rel=>ty_icind.

entry.title: Re: material master before actual costing
entry.content: Create master data and load inventory after CKMSTART. This worked for me till now.

entry.title: Re: create dynamically a type containing an include type
entry.content:
How looks

if_chdo_object_tools_rel=>ty_icdind and

and how looks ztest_abc ?

entry.title: Re: Credit Limit Request Configuration - SAP S4Hana Credit Management
entry.content:
Hi Haran 

Is there a standard way to configure email notifications with an URL navigating to the request raised in case management. 

Detail: 

Your post on credit limit requests is very helpful, in addition to the request i want to send an email with details like

who raised the request?

For which Business partner

what is the limit requestor requested

and a link for approver to navigate to the approval page?

how can this be acheived with out custom development.?

even if we go with custom development is it possible to get the url for the approval page.

 

Please advise, your thoughts on this will be much appreciated.

Thanks 

Loki

entry.title: Re: SAP Crystal Reports, developer version for Microsoft...
entry.content:
You can find End Of Life info here, same as Crystal Reports Designer:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354088411.html

CR for VS follows Crystal Reports Designer, SP 20 is very old, its up to SP 35 now.

You can get the latest here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

NOTE: CR end of life does extend each year for current versions, older versions will eventually have an end of life date.

SAP only supports the current version of CR for VS because it's not patched, only full builds.

SO any issues found you need to duplicate the problem in the latest release.

It's a little confusing for end of life dates on older SP's, there is nothing specific other than staying up to date...

Also, for CR Designer the cycle is 3 current patches are supported, all else require updating.

I kind of recall SP 20 was still CR 2016 days...

All I can suggest is since you are that old it is time to upgrade to the current version to take advantage of all of the current security patches and DB Clients support etc.

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Brazil: Dashboard do Usage Analytics
entry.content:
(English version here)

Olá pessoal,

Lançamos uma nova funcionalidade que permite que você verifique sua utilização do SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada e de faturamento de saída para o Brasil fornecendo informações mais precisas sobre o consumo.

Esse novo recurso só está disponível a partir do ano de 2024 para os IDs de métrica CY140 (faturamento de saída) e CY141 (faturamento de entrada). Para saber qual é o seu ID de métrica, verifique no SAP LUI (License Utilization Information) no app “SAP for Me”. Mais informações sobre SAP LUI estão descritas abaixo.

Você acessa o dashboard do Usage Analytics no painel de navegação esquerdo do SAP BTP Cockpit, como mostrado na imagem a seguir:

No dashboard do Usage Analytics, você pode encontrar informações sobre o produto "SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada e de faturamento de saída para o Brasil" (inbound and outbound invoicing options for Brazil).

Para processos de saída (outbound), temos dois grupos de classificação, chamados Grupo A e Grupo B, como mostrado na imagem a seguir:

Para exibir todas as Usages na tela, deixe o filtro Service em branco. Nem todas as classificações estão sendo exibidas nesta imagem.

Grupo A: Classifica cada documento processado (definição de métrica) por tipo e ação. O Grupo A é composto pelos documentos abaixo.

  • Documentos processados (NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e, MDF-e e NF3-e)
  • Documentos de eventos processados (CC-e, cancelamento, inutilização, etc.)
  • Arquivos de documentos exibidos/baixados*
  • Arquivos de eventos exibidos/baixados*
  • Documentos reenviados para aprovação (NF-e, CT-e, NFS-e, MDF-e e NF3-e)

*Somente quando o mesmo arquivo XML for baixado pela segunda vez. Isso evita o mau consumo.

Acesse o Cloud (SaaS) Application Usage & SAP Business Technology Platform para verificar a lista completa de classificação do Grupo A.

Grupo B: Classifica cada documento processado (definição métrica) conforme o tipo de ambiente:

  • Documentos processados em ambiente produtivo.
  • Documentos processados em ambiente de homologação.

A soma dos documentos exibidos no Grupo A deve ser igual à soma dos documentos exibidos no Grupo B. O número total de documentos é exibido em Total documents from group A or B.

Considerando o exemplo da imagem anterior, a soma do Grupo A é 1317 e a soma do Grupo B também é 1317.

Para processos de fatura de entrada, também temos dois grupos de classificação, chamados Grupo A e Grupo B, como mostrado na imagem a seguir:

Para exibir todas as utilizações na tela, deixe o filtro Service em branco. Nem todas as classificações podem ser exibidas nesta imagem.

Grupo A: Classifica cada documento processado (definição de métrica) por tipo e ação. O grupo A é composto pelos seguintes tipos de documentos:

  • Documentos processados (NF-e, CT-e)
  • Documentos de evento processados (CC-e, cancelamento, etc.)
  • Arquivos de documentos baixados da SEFAZ (NF-e, CT-e)
  • Arquivos de eventos baixados da SEFAZ (CC-e, cancelamento, etc.)

Acesse o Cloud (SaaS) Application Usage e o SAP Business Technology Platform para verificar a lista completa de classificação do Grupo A.

Grupo B: Classifica cada documento processado (definição métrica) conforme o tipo de ambiente:

  • Documentos processados em ambiente produtivo.
  • Documentos processados em ambiente de homologação.

A soma dos documentos exibidos no Grupo A deve ser igual à soma dos documentos exibidos no Grupo B. O número total de documentos é exibido em Total document from group A or B.

Considerando o exemplo da imagem anterior, a soma do Grupo A é 202 e a soma do Grupo B também é 202.

Se você encontrar discrepâncias, certifique-se de que não haja codificação personalizada que chame o serviço em nuvem duas vezes ou crie um efeito de loop.

Principais opções da análise de utilização

  • Capability: no dashboard Usage Analytics, você pode usar o menu suspenso Capability para filtrar a utilização do serviço selecionando os recursos do SAP BTP (aplicativo, automação ou integração).
  • Service: nessa lista suspensa, você pode usá-la para filtrar o uso de cada plano de serviços na subconta da sua empresa. Por exemplo, você pode filtrar a utilização de dados da solução “opção de emissão de fatura de entrada e de faturamento de saída para o Brasil” (inbound and outbound invoicing options for Brazil).
  • Metric: você pode selecionar uma das métricas (documentos exibidos, baixados, reenviados para aprovação ou processados). Você pode utilizar o menu suspenso Metric para evitar exibir todos os documentos ao mesmo tempo.
  • Period: em relação ao intervalo de tempo selecionado no campo Period, esteja ciente de que as informações apresentadas são do ano 2024 em diante e somente após a instalação das seguintes SAP Notes.

Instalação

Você pode implementar essas modificações seguindo as instruções nas SAP Notes.

As SAP Notes são necessárias para fornecer os dados utilizados em métricas e para corresponder à quantidade do Grupo B aos dados de consumo no rótulo Total document from group A or B. A instalação das SAP Notes não tem impacto nos processos de cálculo da fatura atuais.

  • Outbound (faturamento de saída): SAP Note 3111469
  • Inbound (fatura de entrada): SAP Note 3399269

License Utilization Information (LUI)

O aplicativo License Utilization Information (LUI) fornece detalhes sobre a titularidade da licença e sobre a utilização do software. Para receber os resultados, uma medição do sistema é realizada em cada sistema relevante.

O dashboard Usage Analytics pode ser usado em conjunto com a LUI selecionando o mesmo período para gerar análises sobre como a solução SAP Document and Reporting Compliance está sendo usada. O painel do LUI deve mostrar o mesmo valor na coluna Measured e no Total document from group A or B do Usage Analytics.

A aplicação LUI pode ser acessada por meio do SAP for Me. Verifique o License Utilization Information para obter detalhes sobre como utilizá-las e acessá-las.

Data da contabilização

A data da métrica pode não representar a data da ação do usuário. Por exemplo, podem existir discrepâncias em que um documento processado no fim de um mês aparece no consumo do mês seguinte, devido ao processo assíncrono da contabilização. O momento em que um usuário realiza uma ação e quando essa ação é refletida em nossas métricas pode nem sempre se alinhar perfeitamente, especialmente na transição entre os dias.

Você pode entrar em contato com seu executivo de contas se tiver perguntas sobre os valores ou se precisar de mais ajuda. Verifique a SAP Note 2626344.

Para obter um passo a passo sobre como usar o SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada e de faturamento de saída para o Brasil, consulte as seguintes páginas do portal SAP Help:

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil

Você pode receber alerta por email quando novas SAP Notes forem lançadas ou atualizadas nos componentes listados abaixo; basta seguir as instruções do SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-IN
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT
  • LOD-LH-NFE

Acesse o gerenciador de modificações legais para ter uma noção do que está planejado para ser suportado. Consulte o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 e a página “regulatory change manager” para entender como usar esta ferramenta.

Gostou desse post? Dê um Like (Kudos) e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. Sinta-se à vontade para deixar suas perguntas ou ler posts semelhantes no SAP Community do SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Para acompanhar as últimas notícias da NF-e, siga a tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) e a comunidade SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Obrigado e até a próxima,
Bruno Eltz e Thiago Cezimbra

Tags: brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, electronic nota fiscal

entry.title: Display resolution issue with SAP GUI 7.70
entry.content:
When SAP GUI Is launched, it is launching a small SAP Window reducing display resolution of laptop, 

The desktop icons are enlarged and it is very difficult to navigate to options, how do I increase the resolution of SAP that matches computer screen resolution

entry.title: Re: create dynamically a type containing an include type
entry.content: i guess i'm not clear: i have table 1 and a table 2. i need to create dynamically a type1 like types:ty1: include type table1.include type IF_CHDO_OBJECT_TOOLS_REL=>TY_ICDIND. types end of ty1. and for the second: types:ty2: include type table2.include type IF_CHDO_OBJECT_TOOLS_REL=>TY_ICDIND. types end of ty2. since i have 20 tables, i want to create dynamically the type. ho i'm clearer

entry.title: Re: DigiCert SSL certificate configuration for HTTP RFC c...
entry.content:
Hi Sayantan-Pal,

I cannot answer to the specific environment but I will answer based on _my_ personal understanding of how (mutual) SSL certificate handshake works:

  1. The client starts the connection and initiates the SSL handshake
  2. The server offers a server certificate. If the client trusts the issuer of this server certificate, the process continues. Otherwise the client stops with SSL error ("certificate not trusted" or similar).
    => Result: The server has authenticated itself to the client. In case of a normal https connection (w/o client authentication) the SSL handshake setup was complete. Otherwise (if the client has to authenticate itself towards the server), the process continues:
  3. The server sends a list of trustworthy CA certificates to the client
  4. The client checks whether it has a client certificate that was signed by on of these certificates in the list. If so, it is send to the server. If not, the server(?) stops with SSL error ("client did not offer a certificate" or similar)
  5. The server verifies this client certificate against its trusted certificates list. If it fits (like the client pre-selected before), the SSL handshake setup was complete and data transfer can start.

In case of RFC communication with AS ABAP acting as a client, the client usually has an RFC destination in TC SM59 containing the connection details. In this RFC destination, you can select the truststore for your client certificate (=> from TC STRUST). Either "anonymous" (which means "no client authentication" => see step 2 above) or "SSL Client standard" respective any other self-created Client PSE (which means "try to use this certificate for client authentication" => see step 4 above). In case there is a match between the server's trusted certificate list and the provided client PSE, the connections is established.

Conclusion: In case of SSL Client authentication at the end it is the _client_ that decided which certificate it sends to the server and the server decided whether it trust it's issuer.

=> If Your server is located in BTP and SAP decides to remove an old CA from their trusted CA list, you _must_ ensure that your client provides a certificate that is signed by the "new" CA before that point in time. Otherwise you risk an outage.

This works well as server owner usually provide an overlapping period when they trust both the "old" CA and the "new" CA. During that period clients must switch their certificate from "old" to "new".

entry.title: Re: We are currently at ASE SP03 PL11 Should we move Prod...
entry.content:
No definitive answers (at this time) but some suggestions (some of which you may have already addressed) based on how I'd proceed ...

You've stated "more stack traces in SP04( PL04)".  I take this to mean you're also seeing stack traces in SP03 PL11.

First step:

Open a case with tech support for the stack traces in SP03 PL11.  Find out which (if any) newer releases contain fixes to your stack traces.  Also ask the case worker about your SP04 PL04 stack traces ... the case worker should be able to a) tell you if the stack traces are related and/or b) if you should open a new/separate tech support case (ie, you're running into a different issue in SP04 PL04).

Once all stack traces (SP03 PL11, SP04 PL04) have been registered with tech support you should (hopefully) be able to obtain a list of SP03 and/or SP04 releases that contain the desired fixes. (keep in mind some fixes may have to wait for a future release).

Second step:

With a list of (potential) 'good' releases in hand you can start putting together an upgrade plan.  Whether you go with a newer SP03 release or a newer SP04 release will depend on your scenario and your level of risk aversion:

  • if there are newer releases for both SP03 and SP04 that address all stack traces, then I'd opt for the newest SP04 release
  • if the SP03 stack traces are addressed in newer releases but the SP04 stack traces are still pending a fix, then I'd opt for the newest SP03 or SP04 release (where SP03 stack traces are fixed, where SP04 stack traces do occur); alternatively, if you're not in a hurry to upgrade then wait for the SP04 stack traces to be fixed
  • if you're not interested in being SAP's bleeding edge test dummy then remove the latest/greatest SP04 release (PL05 at this point in time) from your list of considerations
  • NOTE: I'm assuming you want all stack traces fixed/addressed; if you can live with some stack traces then this will probably make a couple more releases available for consideration

Third step:

Run the chosen (new) release through your normal dev/test/uat/pre-prod test cycles before thinking about pushing into prod. ("Duh, Mark!" ?)

entry.title: Re: create dynamically a type containing an include type
entry.content:
This method expects a list of data references of structures and builds a large structure containing all these structures. It uses RTTI:

@78\QImporting@ IM_REFERENCES TYPE /COW/T_DREF Table of data references
@7B\QReturning@ value( R_DREF ) TYPE REF TO DATA

DATA: lo_strucdescr TYPE REF TO cl_abap_structdescr,
lo_typedescr TYPE REF TO cl_abap_typedescr,
lt_comp TYPE cl_abap_structdescr=>component_table,
wa_comp LIKE LINE OF lt_comp,
lt_comp_value TYPE TABLE OF /cow/s_comp_tab_value,
wa_comp_value TYPE /cow/s_comp_tab_value,
lv_struc_dref TYPE REF TO data,
ls_message TYPE bapiret2.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <wa_ref> TYPE REF TO data,
<fs_struc_data> TYPE ANY,
<fs_help> TYPE ANY,
<fs_comp> TYPE ANY,
<wa_comp_value> TYPE /cow/s_comp_tab_value.

LOOP AT im_references ASSIGNING <wa_ref>.
* Describe data reference
cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_data_ref(
EXPORTING
p_data_ref = <wa_ref>
RECEIVING
p_descr_ref = lo_typedescr
EXCEPTIONS
reference_is_initial = 1
OTHERS = 2
).
IF sy-subrc NE 0.
ls_message-id = '/COW/MIG_MESS'.
ls_message-type = 'W'.
ls_message-number = '057'.
RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE /cow/cx_root
EXPORTING
gs_message = ls_message.
ENDIF.
* Fill component structure
wa_comp-type ?= lo_typedescr.
wa_comp-name = wa_comp-type->get_relative_name( ).
* Append component
APPEND wa_comp TO lt_comp.
* Fill component structure with values
wa_comp_value-type = wa_comp-type.
wa_comp_value-name = wa_comp-name.
wa_comp_value-value = <wa_ref>.
* Append component
APPEND wa_comp_value TO lt_comp_value.
ENDLOOP.
* Check uniqueness of component names
SORT lt_comp BY name.
DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM lt_comp.
IF LINES( lt_comp ) < LINES( lt_comp_value ).
ls_message-id = '/COW/MIG_MESS'.
ls_message-type = 'W'.
ls_message-number = '058'.
RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE /cow/cx_root
EXPORTING
gs_message = ls_message.
ENDIF.
* Create structure from components
lo_strucdescr = cl_abap_structdescr=>create( lt_comp ).
* Create data from handle
CREATE DATA lv_struc_dref TYPE HANDLE lo_strucdescr.
ASSIGN lv_struc_dref->* TO <fs_struc_data>.

* Fill structure
LOOP AT lt_comp_value ASSIGNING <wa_comp_value>.
ASSIGN <wa_comp_value>-value->* TO <fs_help>.
IF <fs_help> IS ASSIGNED.
ASSIGN COMPONENT <wa_comp_value>-name OF STRUCTURE <fs_struc_data> TO <fs_comp>.
<fs_comp> = <fs_help>.
ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.
* Return structure
r_dref = lv_struc_dref .

entry.title: GRC Process Control: How CCM can be leveraged to monitor HANA Databases
entry.content:
Introduction:

HANA databases often serve as repositories for organization's critical financial data, making them subject to stringent regulatory requirements such as SOX. Leveraging SAP GRC Process Control Continuous Controls Monitoring (CCM) module provides an effective solution to record and monitor database activities in real-time. This article comprehensively covers the steps to setup an automated control in GRC PC for HANA database. I am taking the below real time use case and creating an automated ITGC control in PC to monitor HANA Database and report the exceptions.

 

Use case:

The HANA Database administrators often make configuration changes in HANA DB such as parameters related to memory allocation, disk storage, network settings, security configuration, etc.  Such changes are generally allowed to be happened using SYSTEM account. In this case, there's a need to have a control to review who actually used the SYSTEM account and made the changes to meet the SOX compliance objectives. GRC PC can be leveraged to automate the control monitoring and reporting by following below steps.

 

I am using the standard view "SYS.M_INIFILE_CONTENT_HISTORY" that stores the database global parameters (refer below screenshot). The field USER_NAME shows the actual database user id who made the change and APPLICATION_USER_NAME is the one who used the DB user (USER_NAME) to make the change and APPLICATION_NAME shows the application where the change is initiated from like HDBStudio, HANa Cockpit or from OS level etc.

 

Let's say only the SYSTEM user is allowed to make configuration changes. The control requirement is to review the logs at the end of each month and report any changes made by the users other than SYSTEM.

Let's define a control in PC and automate monitoring. To achieve it, perform below steps.

  1. Create a calculation view in HANA database to list the configuration changes and send it to process control.
  2. Establish a connection between PC and HANA Database and assign the connector to Integration scenario "AM"
  3. Create a Data source in PC
  4. Create a Business Rule in PC
  5. Create an automated Control in PC and assign the owner (agent)
  6. Assign business rule to the control.
  7. Schedule an automated monitoring job

 

Pre-requisites:

  • GRC system - At least on 10.1 (Please refer to SAP Product Availability Matrix for more information)
  • GRC Plugin to be installed on HANA Database - Delivery Unit Deployment (Ref: 2589878 - Installing or upgrading HANA GRC Plug-in - SAP for Me)

 

  • Create a calculation view in HANA database:

GRC process control can read only the calculation view result returned from the Hana data base. So, you need to create a calculation view in Hana database by including the columns from the view to be returned. (Best practice is to minimize the amount of data to be passed to PC by applying conditions and calculations at HANA database side rather than passing large chunk of data to PC and apply the filters and calculations there).

Limitations:

  1. PC supports only "SQL Script" type calculation view.
  2. PC supports only four datatypes - DATE, INTEGER, NVARCHAR and DECIMAL. If any fields of other datatype, their type to be converted to one of the supported data type.
  3. Calculation view must have at least a measure field.

Here's a sample script for calculation view.

 

 Note: Customers who have Enterprise HANA is in place (do not allow development on HANA tenant databases) can achieve this by creating virtual tables. (I am going to cover this with another use case very soon)

  • Establish a connection between PC and HANA Database:
    • Create HANA Database connector in GRC system using the transaction code DBCO
    • Create a connector using SM59 (type: "L")
    • Define Connector and Connector group in GRC and assign the connector to connector group.
    • Maintain Connection settings - assign the Hana DB connector to the integration scenario (AM - automated monitoring")

The remaining steps are common for all automated controls. I am going to cover those steps in a separate blog.

Hope you enjoyed reading this blog. In case of any queries, let me know in comment or get in touch with me at LinkedIn or email.

entry.title: Re: create dynamically a type containing an include type
entry.content: In fact it also fills the structures. But you do not need to use that part of the code. You can use RTTI with describe_by_name.

entry.title: EPM_OIA_APPS not found
entry.content:
Hi,

I need to activate EPM_OIA_APPS which is a set of Fiori Demo apps and I do not know how to do it, does anyone please have a hint?

Regards

Mauricio

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere is ready to take over the role of SAP BW
entry.content:
Thanks for all the comments. 

First of all. The idea is not a big bang and replace BW 7.5 over short period of time. I acknowledge that there are still gaps. And I hope that they are closed. 

The suggestion is to have BW 7.5 and Datasphere side by side until the support of BW7.5 comes to an end in Dec 2027. That gives 3,5 years and approx 14 releases to close the necessary gaps that still exists. 

All new requirements that can be classified as low to medium complexity should be built in Datasphere native. Low to medium complex solution from the BW 7.5 can gradually be re-built in Datasphere side. For high complex solution which requires functionality with known gaps, the customers should stay on BW7.5 until the gap is closed. 

With this approach customer does not have to spend money on upgrading to BW/4HANA, instead use the coming 3.5 years to slowly migrate to SAP Datasphere. I feel confident that within 3.5 years the "must have" gaps are closed. 

Secondly, everyone working with SAP BW should ask oneselves why are SAP BW losing Market Share, why are company after company decommissioning or at least have a plan to decommission SAP BW? Solutions can be built in another way than in BW. And there are a lot of companies that do not use BW for reporting, even on SAP data and they manage to serve their users with analytics and reporting capabilities.  

Thirdly. In the recent Datasphere sizing the Storage Max is 7,2 TB and Compute 1,8 TB. I strongly believe that many BW 7.5 systems contains obsolete solutions, redundant data non-optimized modeling patterns, hence with current sizing of SAP Datasphere should be sufficient for many customers. 

Fourthly, BW bridge is not mentioned because it is as the name suggests a bridge. I think it is better to do this approach and migrate slowly but firmly over 3,5 years. To me BW Bridge is a temporary step towards Datasphere native. 

Br

Kenneth

entry.title: Re: Testing SAP Build Process Automation task UI in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
@Mark112358 

Yes, I can retrieve data from Northwind OData service.

To achieve this, add an OData service using Service Manager.

This will add necessary settings to the task ui project. Then, copy the setting in ui5.yaml to the ui5.yaml of the wrapper.

The ui5.yaml file of the taskui-wrapper will look like the image below:

entry.title: COR6N Yield UoM
entry.content:
Hi All, 

In the material master, Base Unit is BT (Bottle) and the conversion is defined between PAL and BT.    

Master recipe is defined with the same BT unit only and Operation/Phase unit is also BT.  

During Process Order confirmation [COR6N] as soon as I select order number and Phase number then the wrong Unit of measure for Yield is populated by-default. (e.g. PAL for palette) 

Now, If I manually delete this default unit from the COR6N screen and when I press the enter key then the correct unit (e.g. BT for bottle) is getting appeared from the master recipe's operation. 

Question is why COR6N screen picks up PAL unit by default?

Hi,

This message is like you have not entered the User Status correctly in mass change. Try to go to the dropdown in the mass change and choose the user status you want to update.

entry.title: Re: development, quality, production -- these are called...
entry.content:
Hi,

Systems or Landscape or Environments are SAP terminology during discussion etc. All represents the same.

Calling it has servers is very rare.

Regards

Terence

entry.title: Re: Remove blank lightbox pop-up in SAP Companion
entry.content:
Hi,

It is probably a link tile that has been set to auto start.  To turn it off:

1. Open SAP Companion and click the editing pencil to go into editing mode
2. Open the relevant link tile for editing
3. Open the Link section of properties and turn off the Show as Announcement setting
4. Save the change and publish the content

Regards
Shane

entry.title: How to define Action Box in Public Cloud (e.g. print 8D Report)
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are trying to define some activities in the action box in the public cloud. I've seen that it is possible now to use some badis (FOA_EXECUTION_FOR_TASK) to create own follow up actions and enable the use of them in the action box as activities. In on premise it was possible to use function modules (e.g. QM06_FM_TASK_CREATE_8DREPORT) and link them as activities in the action box. How can we reproduce this in public cloud?

entry.title: Re: Clean code checks for ABAP – Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Hi @OlgaDolinskaja,

Thanks for your reply. I tried installing Code Pal for the Cloud today in our on-premise central check system (which was recently upgraded S/4HANA 2023 / 7.58). I was able to create the online repo and make the pull request okay, but when I try to activate the checks in SCI, I'm getting an error stating that "check category CL_CI_CATEGORY_WRAPPED is not yet active." From what I can see, these new checks are assigned to check category /CC4A/CODE_PAL, not CL_CI_CATEGORY_WRAPPED. CL_CI_CATEGORY_WRAPPED doesn't appear to exist in our system. Have you seen this error before or do you have any suggestions as to what the problem might be?

Thanks again for you help!
Scott

entry.title: SAC Data Action Advanced Formula - Calculate flexible Average amount
entry.content:
The following calculation should be created in Data Action.

The values of Working Days (Key Figure) and Activity (Dimension + Key Figure Quantity) are copied from the Actual Version to the FC Version

The quantity should be calculated for the forecast (period 10-12).

Example 1:  calculation for period 9 and activity A

Total Activity A from periods 1-9 (360) divided by the sum of days in periods 1-9 (188) * number of days in period 9

(360/188)*21 =40

The user should use the variable input to determine the periods for which the FC values should be calculated.

Example 2:  Actual values are only copied up to period 6

The quantity should be calculated for the forecast (period 7-12).

Example: Calculation for Activity A and Period 7

(210/123)*22=38

Has anyone faced a similar problem?

Thank you!

Mar

entry.title: Re: Can I delete any Resource line from product tree by S...
entry.content:
Dear Ankit CHAUHAN,

I use below php code but I did not understand where should I add " B1S-ReplaceCollectionsOnPatch=true" statement?

 

curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST, 'PATCH');
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POST, true);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, $postData);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_TIMEOUT, 59);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYHOST, 0);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_SSL_VERIFYPEER, 0);
curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_COOKIE, $strCookie);

 

I found the solution. I should add below line and it worked like a charm.

curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, ['B1S-ReplaceCollectionsOnPatch:true']);

 

Regards,

Mucip:)

entry.title: Re: Mexico DRC - Invoice rejected - schema xml issue
entry.content:
Hello,

Please check if you have updated the flow. Recently SAP Product team has released new iflow version to accommodate Comercio Exterior 2.0:

Some of the changes with the new were:

1. Version has been updated from 1.1 to 2.0

2. TipoOperacion was removed

The error message indicates you may still be using an older iflow version.

entry.title: How Skill Library AI Creator works (20241H release)
entry.content:
I have three questions about the AI-powered skill library creation that was announced for the 2024 1H release.

1. Is it correct that the Skill Foundation, one of the three components of TIH (Growth Portfolio, Attribute Library, Skill Foundation), is responsible for creating the skill library using job requirements and job role information by using machine learning capabilities?

2. Will the AI automatically generate the library based on job requirements and job role information pre-loaded by customers in EC? Or will it be built using 3K job requirement data from external organizations? Alternatively, will it be a combination of both?

3. Where can customers access the AI-created skill library? Will the data automatically flow into the Attribute Library?

Thank you for your time and insights.

entry.title: Disable HTTP in Solution Manager
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

Due to security concerns we want to disable HTTP completely in our SAP Solution Manager system. Whereas we found a note 2952317 indicating this is not possible.

Point 4. Even if you are using HTTPS protocol, the ABAP stack must know the HTTP protocol as well.

Can you please confirm if this is still true, that it is not possible to disable HTTP in Solution Manager system?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Disable HTTP in Solution Manager
entry.content:
Hi ,

Yes, as the KBA 2952317 mentioned, from the Solution Manager Configuration perspective, please enable the HTTP protocol on the solution manager ABAP side. You can redirect the HTTP protocol to the HTTPS protocol due to security concerns.

If you still want to disable the HTTP protocol through some other methods but encounter errors when running the SICF service, you can contact BC-MID-ICF.

Best regards,
Leo
SAP Support

entry.title: Mass management of handling units in STO
entry.content:
We need to manage the transfer of approximately 500/DIGITS packages within the STO. When creating the outbound delivery, we are not able to massively select the HUs to be moved but we must select the HUs one by one. Isn't there a massive selection of HUs to be moved? Currently this method represents an increase in the execution times of the activity and is not appreciated by the customer. On 04/17 we have GoLive and we need to present a valid alternative solution. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Mass management of handling units in STO
entry.content:
Hi, 

 

If the issue storage location is not EWM relevant, you can consider assign HU with APP Outbound Deliveries for Handling Units - Documents due for Delivery(VL10HU).

Below is the SAP Help portal page for your reference.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/9de8c353b677b44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

  • Assign handling units to open deliveries.

    You select handling units for orders and assign them directly to open deliveries. The system updates the delivery with the cumulated material items and assigns the handling units to the delivery. For more information, see How to Assign Handling Units to Existing Deliveries.

 

Best Regards,

entry.title: Business area
entry.content: Hi SAP Gurus, I have a query regarding business area. We would like to change the business area from 1000 to 3000. Can anyone please suggest how this transition takes place or will there be any challenges or any impact on existing SAP data base till Mar'24.

entry.title: Re: Pricing - Volume discount by item group total
entry.content:
Hey J, I hate to be a "negative Nancy" here but there is just no way out-of-the-box to do this.

In North America this is called "Family Pricing" and it requires something like B1UP to calculate it.  Usually I'll do a button on the sidebar and then use a custom DI API function to group up the family quantities and do the discounting.

Out-of-the-box is just the single item code, single discount scenario you have identified.

It is definitely doable and I have done it a few times but it's all custom work, hopefully you have B1UP?

Mike

entry.title: I have a confusion regarding SAP Community ID
entry.content:
Namskar ma'am or sir
I have a confusion that SAP Community ID is the same as SAP Universal ID ?

Or
We have received a mail where we can access a user ID like
PXXXXXXXXXXX
is it a community ID ?

entry.title: Re: In SAP B1, is it possible to run Depreciation run job via DI API or Service Layer?
entry.content:
I don't know about the depreciation run specifically but looking at the SDK Guide for V10 you can use the AssetDocument Object. I'm guessing this could be used to make depreciations. With the enumerations you can do ordinary depreciation, scrapping, etc.

I am relatively new to the DI API stuff and I don't have a specific example but the object seems to be there to do it.

entry.title: Re: Scope ID 40Y Intercompany Reconciliation will not hel...
entry.content:
No, ICMR doesn't support clearing.

Clearing and matching are two different activities. Clearing is triggered when the actual payment happens; it reconciles an invoice and a payment within a company. However, matching is to match a customer invoice and a vendor invoice that belong to two different companies.

There is no correlation between the two activities. One cannot say if a customer invoice was created on the seller side and a corresponding vendor invoice was created on the buy side, and then you can clear both invoices with no knowledge of whether the actual payments happened.

entry.title: Re: Business area
entry.content:
Hi @AK6 

It would have helped if you had shared the business scenario where you would want to change the business area and also the system version as well as additional details  (Document splitting with Business Area as one of the criterion). Also do you have special ledger as well in your system for reporting purposes.

Let us take a simple example of material document, a change in business area could be effected as answered earlier:

Changing Business Area in a Material Document - SAP Community

You have to be mindful of the financial statements, since many customers use business area to comply with IFRS 8 - Segmental Reporting and a change just implemented through configuration change can throw your financial reports for a toss. It would be helpful if you share bit more details for the change and why you want do such a change, when the change is being implemented (Eg, fiscal year end etc).

Thanks & regards

Sanil Bhandari

Join here Bengaluru - SAP Community Groups and get updates on the latest local community posts and events. Feel free to start a discussion if you want to.

entry.title: OpenText VIM Issue: Create Doc. BG Failure
entry.content:
Hello,

I am facing an issue where, for all invoices, the process is throwing an error for the activity "Create Doc. BG" status as unsuccessful.

While trying to invoice this DP in the workplace it gives a message "No data found in table /OPT/VIM_1HEAD for requested conditions"

This is happening for all the invoices we are trying to process in #VIM

Appreciate any feedback on how we can resolve this.

entry.title: Re: Approval Query based on department and based on amount
entry.content:
Hi Wale!  You should be able to do this just with the CASE statements you are using:

SELECT CASE WHEN $[OPOR.U_UserDept] = 'ADD' AND $[OPOR.DocCur] = '' (<--currency code for Naira) AND $[OPOR.DocTotalFC] BETWEEN 0 AND 5000 THEN 'TRUE' WHEN $[OPOR.U_UserDept] = 'ADD' AND $[OPOR.DocCur] = '' (<--code for system currency) AND $[OPOR.DocTotal] BETWEEN 0 AND 1000001 THEN 'TRUE' ELSE 'FALSE' END

(something like this above, not exactly)

You could also just do two approval templates if you want different approvers but using the same fields and logic.  If you just make your specific CASE clauses have the values you want then make the query resolve to 'TRUE' you should be OK.

On the PO when you use any FC (Naira I would assume is a foreign currency in your scenario) it already will do the conversion by the exchange rate and show the total in the $[OPOR.DocTotalFC] dynamic syntax field.  (and even with FC if you use the regular [OPOR.DocTotal] it should give you the system currency equivalent of the FC value at the daily exchange rate too).

I hope this gives you what you need to finish the customization.  I am not on this forum very much (I see you tagged me) but somehow I'm getting notifications.

You can message me on LinkedIn (http://linkedin.battleshipcobra.com/) if you need more help, thanks!

M

entry.title: Re: Upgrade SAP Business Application Studio to SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Thank you for the right-time blog

Well, I have the below queries:

2. Since my org. is still on Suite on HANA so, for Fiori App Development can we go for service say SAP BAS (standalone) or SAP Build Code (with SAP BAS)? My Org. is planning to take a subscription but we are confused about how SAP Build Code is going to benefit us, whether we should go SAP BAS or SAP Build Code!! 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: 入門！SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning 機能紹介シリーズ - データロック
entry.content:
Spoiler
ただし、データロックはパブリックバージョンの削除を防ぐものではないため、その目的でデータアクセス制御を利用する必要があります。

ちょっとここは理解できてないのですが、編集権限もないのに、どうしてPublic Versionを削除できますか。

entry.title: Re: BP master data show active, but the list of BP and qu...
entry.content:
OCRD.frozenFor = 'N' means the BP is "Active".

This is the field I commonly use to indicate if the BP is inactive, but it's backwards to what you think.  IE, frozenFor = 'Y' is "Inactive".

Without seeing your database, maybe you have an active or inactive date range?  But in general for query purposes I only use "frozenFor" (Y/N) as my inactive/active indicator.

Let me know if this makes sense,

M

Company oCompany = new Company();
oCompany.Server = "Live_ServerT";
oCompany.CompanyDB = "Max";
oCompany.UserName = "manager";
oCompany.Password = "Max@234";
oCompany.DbUserName = "sa";
oCompany.DbPassword = "sa2014";
oCompany.DbServerType = BoDataServerTypes.dst_MSSQL2014;

entry.title: Re: Error during Maintenance order confirmation in HANA 2022 Version

entry.title: Count Top Skip are not supported in Odata Query of RAP service binding
entry.content:
Hi, RAP Expert,

I run into failure in the first query. Would you please help me on this how to resolve it ?

 

REQUEST

I tried annotation with ObjectModel.query.implementedBy:'ABAP:ZCL_FO_QUEQRY' and the code is in the following box. But it doesn't resolve the issue .

CLASS zcl_fo_queqry DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    INTERFACES if_rap_query_provider .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS zcl_fo_queqry IMPLEMENTATION.


  METHOD if_rap_query_provider~select.

              DATA(lv_offset) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_offset( ).
              DATA(lv_page_size) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_page_size( ).
              DATA(lv_max_rows) = COND #( WHEN lv_page_size = if_rap_query_paging=>page_size_unlimited
                                          THEN 0 ELSE lv_page_size ).
              DATA(lt_req_elements) = io_request->get_requested_elements( ).
              DATA(lv_req_elements)  = concat_lines_of( table = lt_req_elements sep = `, ` ).
              DATA(lv_sql_filter) = io_request->get_filter( )->get_as_sql_string( ).

  TRY.


  CASE io_request->get_entity_id( ).
  WHEN 'ZR_FORDER_ITEMS' .

              IF io_request->is_data_requested( ).

              DATA lt_fo_items_response TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ZR_FORDER_ITEMS.
               SELECT (lv_req_elements) FROM ZR_Forder_ITEMS
               WHERE (lv_sql_filter)
               ORDER BY transportation_order_item
               INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @LT_fo_items_response
               OFFSET @LV_offset UP TO @LV_max_rows ROWS.
               io_response->set_data( it_data = lt_fo_items_response  ).
*               CATCH cx_rap_query_response_set_twic.

             ENDIF .

           IF io_request->is_total_numb_of_rec_requested( ).
**select count
              SELECT COUNT( * ) FROM ZR_FORDER_ITEMS
                                WHERE (lv_sql_filter)
                                INTO @DATA(lv_count).
**fill response
              io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lv_count ).
            ENDIF.

  WHEN 'ZR_FREIGHTORDER' .
  IF io_request->is_data_requested( ).

              DATA lt_fo_headers_response TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ZR_FREIGHTORDER.

               SELECT (lv_req_elements) FROM ZR_FREIGHTORDER
               WHERE (lv_sql_filter)
               ORDER BY transportationorder
               INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @LT_fo_headers_response
               OFFSET @LV_offset UP TO @LV_max_rows ROWS.

               io_response->set_data( it_data = lt_fo_headers_response  ).



  ENDIF.

             IF io_request->is_total_numb_of_rec_requested( ).
**select count
              SELECT COUNT( * ) FROM ZR_FREIGHTORDER
                                WHERE (lv_sql_filter)
                                INTO @DATA(lv_count1).
**fill response
              io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lv_count1 ).
            ENDIF.

  ENDCASE.

        CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(exception).
        DATA(exception_message) = cl_message_helper=>get_latest_t100_exception( exception )->if_message~get_longtext( ).

  ENDTRY.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

Best Regards!

Jacky Liu

entry.title: Unable to load metadata of fiori apps in WebIDE.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I'm unable to load metadata of fiori apps in WebIDE, but when we cross-check this issue in Development, Quality and Production metadata gets load. so facing this issue locally in WebIDE. see the below error which gets display while loading metadata.

error :

javax.net.ssl.SSLHandshakeException: Received fatal alert: certificate_expired

Please look into this issue and provide solution asap.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK attachment multi image selection at a time fo...
entry.content:
Hi Jitendra,

Thanks for your response. Could you please help any workaround( sample code we can refer)

Thanks,

Krishna 

@Jitendra_Kansal @bill_froelich

entry.title: Re: Automatic payment program not picking up a vendor
entry.content:
Hi,

In f110, go to the run in which you get the error.

Go to edit, proposal- error list.

It should show the error reason.

Either vendor is blocked or vendor is already present in another proposal run for which payment is not completed.

entry.title: SAP CPI - UDF Mapping Context Issue
entry.content:
Hello Experts of CPI,

I have a requirement of mapping and below is the requirement which i am unable to get the output, please review and provide the best CPI UDF solution

I used create copies udf but it is not creating as shown in target value needed

1 source context values should repeat again and again by given count value

Source single context values     context number of copies             Target Value Needed

101.22                                                   3                                             101.22

202.22                                                   2                                            202.22

303.22                                                   1                                            303.22

                                                                                                             ------------

                                                                                                             101.22

                                                                                                             202.22

                                                                                                             ------------

entry.title: Re: Test drive Joule's generative AI features in SAP Build Code now!
entry.content:
I followed the steps for creating a project in SAP Build Code but could not proceed to Step2 due to error in with SAP Build Code Test Drive, at the time when the project opens in SAP Business Application Studio, the SAP Build Code development environment:

"Error loading webview: Error: Could not register service worker: SecurityError: Failed to register a ServiceWorker: The provided scriptURL ('https://theia-workspaces-ws-rrh95.us10.trial.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/oss-808fca1916a139c8019f2822376f5883bbe282ee/static/out/vs/workbench/contrib/webview/browser/pre/service-worker.js?v=4&vscode-resource-base-authority=vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net&remoteAuthority=theia-workspaces-ws-rrh95.us10.trial.applicationstudio.cloud.sap') violates the Content Security Policy.."

Joule also does not show anything:

entry.title: Re: SAP Solution Capabilities for Circular Battery Management
entry.content:
Would it be possible to get a clearer version of some of the pictures used in this post? Specifically the second two images.

@sscharfenberger 

Regards

entry.title: How to define ajax call in SAP MDK
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We have a requirement where we need to call ajax get and post calls.

We are trying the below code( SAP UI5 code)in a rule but its was not working

Here URL is we are using Mobile service Registration URL under API section.

 

export default function PostEmailIdDetails () {
$.ajax({
url: "https://XXXXX",
method: "POST",
success: onRegSuccess,
error: onRegError
});
}

We need to define ajax call to update the email id in the mobile service user registration.

Please advise how we need to call?.

@bill_froelich jitendra kansal

Thanks,

Krishna.

entry.title: Re: is it possible to fill variable on material?
entry.content:
Assuming that your table is describing two different materials, you might look into informative characteristics along with characteristic profiles.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9905622a5c1f49ba84e9076fc83a9c2c/5632c353b677b44ce10000000a174cb4.html

This should as well be supported e.g. in F4 Help (Material by characteristic value).

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Application Model - Page Map not loading
entry.content: This worked for me, thank you

entry.title: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 2 - Formulas
entry.content:
Now the challenge gets real 😯

I’ve heard that many developers – citizen and otherwise -- pretty much understand the idea of formulas, and can modify existing formulas and create basic formulas. But even those around SAP Build Apps for a while still struggle to come up with complex formulas from scratch. This challenge will take you to the next level.

The challenges so far:

  • Task 0: Set Up SAP Build Apps
  • Task 1: OData 
  • Task 2: Formulas (this post)

The Real Purpose of Formulas

Essentially, formulas are a way to transform data – to convert data to a different data type, to aggregate, to reduce, to format.

So given that, we need to first understand the different “types” of data.

 

Type of Data

Before beginning, it helps to know the basics of the JSON format, a text-based data interchange format. There are plenty of sites for learning JSON, such as this one: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/JavaScript/Objects/JSON  

Despite it looking complicated, essentially, all the data you deal with will be one of the following:

  • Primitive: String, number, Boolean, null

 

"My string"
100
True
null

 

  • Object: A single collection of fields, each of which is a primitive, list or another object

 

{a: “My String”, b: 100, c: True}
{a: 100, b: {c: “subobject”} }
{a: 100, b: [1, "Two", { x: 3 } ]}

 

  • Lists: A set of primitives, objects or (sub)-lists

 

[1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]

[
   {salesOrderID: 1234, NetAmount: 100.33},
   {salesOrderID: 9999, NetAmount: 3240.99}
]

[1, [2, 3], 4, "five", 6, {"next": 7} ]

 

These 3 “types” can be combined in all sorts of ways to get interesting data, but essentially that’s all there is.

 

Tools for Transforming Data

The formula editor is the tool you use to build formulas. Inside here, you have many functions to transform data from one type to another.

For example, you search for functions that work on lists – these functions will take lists and transform them in some way. Take the MAP function. The documentation inside the formula editor is very good – it will provide a very good explanation of what the function does, with examples that you can actually modify and see how the function works.

The description tells you what type of data you start with and what type you end up with. Here, for MAP, it says it takes a list, and returns a list, but the contents of the list are transformed.

Or take SUM, which takes a list of numbers and returns a number..

Any function can be thought of this way: What type of data does it take, and what type of data does it return.

 

Strategy for Formulas (Example)

When you need to transform data, you should know what you are starting with and what you want to end up with, then figure out the intermediary steps for the transformation. Finally you have to find the functions that can do the type of transformations you need.

For example, let’s say you want to configure a dropdown list so the user can select a number that is in the range of 1 to some number, and that number is stored in a page variable.

 

pageVars.options

 

But the dropdown control needs a list of objects in this format:

 

[
{label: "1", value: "1"},
{label: "2", value: "2"},
{label: "3", value: "3"}
]

 

So, you need to come up with a strategy for transforming the primitive into a list of objects. Here’s one way to do this, step by step.

  • Since you need a list, you can use the GENERATE_RANGE function to transform the single number (primitive) to a list of numbers:

 

GENERATE_RANGE(1, pageVars.options)

 

This gets you this result.

 

[1,2,3,4,5]

 

  • But instead of a list of primitives, you need a list of objects. So you can use the MAP function to transform the items in the list -- because you know the MAP function transforms from one type of list to another type of list.

 

MAP(GENERATE_RANGE(1, pageVars.options),{label: item, value: item })

 

MAP iterates over your original list, and provides you the variable item that holds the current item in the list.

This gets you this result.

 

[
   {label: 1, value: 1},
   {label: 2, value: 2}
]

 

  • But the label and value fields must be provided as strings, so you will do another transformation, changing one type of primitive to another type of primitive. Here, you can use the STRING function to transform a number to a string.

 

MAP(GENERATE_RANGE(1, pageVars.options),{label: STRING(item), value: STRING(item)})

 

This gets you this result, which is what you need for your dropdown list.

 

[
   {label: "1", value: "1"},
   {label: "2", value: "2"},
   {label: "3", value: "3"},
   {label: "4", value: "4"},
   {label: "5", value: "5"}
]

 

 

Your Task

Part I

Using the app you worked on from Task 1, change the pagination so it returns 20 items per page.

Then add a button with the text Sum of Top 20.

When the user taps the button, calculate the sum of the first 20 sales items using the data variable, which holds that data. Format the sum with a dollar sign, commas, and 2 decimal places, no spaces (there is a function that does this for you). Your number should look something like this (your amount will differ):

 

$123,456.99.

 

Go to the Submit page, hash the string.

NOTE: If you need help with the formula, just take it one step at a time. If needed, post in this thread for assistance.

 

Part II

You will use another formula, but this time to chart the data. Go to the Marketplace by clicking on Marketplace at the top of the UI components. Take some time to explore all the cool components (and flow functions) available.

Find the pie chart component and install it.

Just below the button, add a container, make it white, and then add a pie chart to the container. At this point it should look like this, since you have no data:

Now you will set the data for the pie chart with a formula. Go to the Chart data property of the pie chart and create a formula to display the top 6 sales order items. Use:

  • Material for the display text
  • NetAmount for the data value

It should look something like this (your data will be different):

👉 When you are done, take a screen shot, reply to this thread, and post the hash from Part I and the screenshot from Part II.
